message:
perf(stats): avoid git process on cached activity

The stats recent-activity cache still paid for a git rev-parse subprocess on every hot-cache run just to rediscover repo root and HEAD. Read normal Git metadata directly for standard repositories and worktrees, including loose and packed refs, and keep the existing git command path as fallback.

Also resolve the git executable once for uncached git log fallback calls so long agent PATHs do not trigger repeated failed exec attempts.

Measured on the copied current .beads DB with release-perf under the same later-load window: stats --json hot-cache 36.1 ms +/- 2.2 -> 33.2 ms +/- 1.7. Process trace now shows no child git execs on the cached path, and stats JSON checksum stayed a3762e6e9460afc5b604b4b25aa0125ff5fc7712cf9f357aa5dbd007f028173a.
